title: Mushi-Shi
author: Artland
comment: My memory with Mushi-shi was fuzzy. I was given a medium to watch, and the first thing I saw was this anime. The exploration of the supernatural in an uncanny, yet calm and soothing, as if the Mushi themselves also live a life like we do. Ginko, the protagonist, blends himself within the world of two living beings, and crafting life lessons from what he has experienced, even in life and death situations.

title: Kill la Kill
author: TRIGGER
comment: This anime awakened something in me when I was young. I forgot how to articulate this series, but I remember it was peak, and it's all.

title: Takopi no Genzai
author: Enishiya
comment: I feel like short stories like these are hardly seen by most people, especially this one. This one is brave enough to daringly tackle the absurdity of life under abuse, neglect, and mental fuckups. I went into this series drunk as hell, watching with 5 other homies, and walked out sobbing like a bitch. Would definitely, heavily recommend to others.

title: Shingeki no Kyoujin
author: Wit/MAPPA
comment: The only I remember from the anime after dropping during Shiganshina arc was Eren glowing up to become The Rumbler and killed 8 billion people on earth. Some said the ending sucks, I thought to myself: 'Maybe not investing in the anime isn't so bad after all'
